channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2025 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2025 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Achieving trap-depth-tunable organic persistent luminescence through host energy-level engineering
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 23 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67529-3

Zhan et al. report a host energy level engineering strategy to modulate trap depth in organic host-guest systems, achieving persistent luminescence up to 27 hours and energy storage for 14 days. 4×4 electroluminescent arrays based on the host-guest system demonstrate pixel-programmable information storage capabilities.
item.dc:title: Achieving trap-depth-tunable organic persistent luminescence through host energy-level engineering
item.dc:creator: Chenhan Zhan
Cunjian Lin
Rujun Yang
Yajing Wang
Zishuang Wu
Yang Li
Wenting Deng
Jumpei Ueda
Yixi Zhuang
Rong-Jun Xie

item.title: Flexible monolithic 3D complementary circuits based on 2D semiconductor inks
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 22 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-66419-y

2D semiconductors hold promise for the fabrication of high-density flexible integrated circuits, but they often require high-temperature processing or transfer steps. Here, the authors report the low-temperature ( ≤ 150 °C) fabrication of wafer-scale 3Dintegrated flexible complementary circuits based on 2D semiconductor inks.
item.dc:title: Flexible monolithic 3D complementary circuits based on 2D semiconductor inks
item.dc:creator: Taoyu Zou
Seongmin Heo
Youjin Reo
Gi-Seong Ryu
Jaeyun Lee
Mingyu Kim
Soonhyo Kim
Gwon Byeon
Ji-Sang Park
Wentao Xu
Yong-Young Noh

item.title: Unconventional high-harmonic generation in resonant membrane metasurfaces
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 22 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67871-6

High-harmonic generation from metasurfaces is believed to follow conventional power laws with integer exponents. In this work, authors discover that the strong local fields related to a metasurface high-Q resonances can modify the effective nonlinear response and enable the generation of high harmonics following non-integer-power laws.
item.dc:title: Unconventional high-harmonic generation in resonant membrane metasurfaces
item.dc:creator: Pavel Tonkaev
Felix Richter
Ivan Toftul
Maria Antonietta Vincenti
Ivan Sinev
Michael Scalora
Hatice Altug
Yuri Kivshar
